notes: Path B: Carry / Volatility Risk Premium / Cross-Asset hypotheses.
Separate ledger from main (trial_ledger.json) because economic mechanisms are fundamentally different.
Stopping rule: 3 consecutive REJECT → stop this edge class.
Factor-Control rule: R² > 0.30 with DXY/VIX/SPX = reject (confounding factor, not edge).
Parameters are FROZEN at registration. No tuning after backtests.

hypotheses.name: Carry Trade — Interest Rate Differential on XAUUSD
hypotheses.hypothesis: Gold appreciates when US real rates fall relative to global rates. Short US rates = long gold via carry mechanism.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Interest rate differentials drive capital flows. When US rates decline relative to global rates, gold becomes more attractive as a non-yielding asset with negative opportunity cost.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: Volatility Risk Premium — Implied vs Realized Vol Spread on Gold
hypotheses.hypothesis: Gold mean-reverts when implied vol (GVZ) exceeds realized vol (VRP > 0). Gold trends when realized vol exceeds implied (VRP < 0).
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Volatility risk premium is compensation for bearing tail risk. When GVZ >> realized, market overpays for insurance → sell premium via mean-reversion. When realized >> GVZ, regime shift → follow trend.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: Cross-Asset Momentum — DXY Lead-Lag on XAUUSD
hypotheses.hypothesis: DXY z-score extremes predict XAUUSD follow-through over 1-5 days. Retail/CTA flow on gold reprices slower than interbank DXY flow.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Interbank DXY flow moves first, retail/CTA gold flow follows with delay. The lag is tradeable if persistent across regimes.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: DXY Divergence — Gold-USD Partial Conviction Signal
hypotheses.hypothesis: Gold and USD divergence with partial conviction (0.5 strength) generates positive risk-adjusted returns via ATR-sized stops.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Gold-USD divergence reflects real-rate expectations. Partial conviction reduces false signals while maintaining exposure to the structural relationship.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: Time-Series Momentum — Multi-Timeframe Trend Consensus
hypotheses.hypothesis: Gold exhibits time-series momentum across 1m/3m/12m horizons. Weighted consensus signal captures persistent trend component.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Moskowitz et al. (2012) documented time-series momentum across asset classes. Gold trends persist due to slow-moving institutional flows and regime persistence.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: FOMC Drift — Post-Announcement Directional Persistence
hypotheses.hypothesis: Gold continues moving in the direction of the initial FOMC-day reaction for 1-5 days. Post-FOMC drift is tradeable with fixed hold period.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: FOMC announcements cause information asymmetry. Slow information diffusion among different investor classes creates predictable drift in the 1-5 day window.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress

hypotheses.name: COT Positioning — Managed Money Contrarian Signal
hypotheses.hypothesis: Extreme Managed Money net positioning in gold futures predicts mean reversion. Crowded positioning = no marginal buyer/seller left.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: CFTC Disaggregated COT data reveals hedge fund/CTA positioning. Extreme positioning indicates crowded trades with limited remaining capital to push prices further.
hypotheses.validation_plan: edge_search → dk_test → label_shuffle → ml_filter → factor_control → cost_realistic → bootstrap → stress
